stemHtml: How does an intercepting aircraft signal "YOU MAY PROCEED" to the intercepted aircraft?

options.a: By executing an abrupt break-away manoeuvre consisting of a climbing turn of 90° or more without crossing the intercepted aircraft's flight path.
options.b: By rocking wings and flashing navigation lights at regular intervals.
options.c: By rocking wings twice ahead of the intercepted aircraft, followed by a slow level turn (normally left) after acknowledgement.
options.d: By circling the intercepted aircraft anti-clockwise (aeroplanes) or clockwise (helicopters).
